creator: jhein@symmetricom.com
text:
sunpoet@FreeBSD.org wrote at 19:54 +0000 on Nov 20, 2013:
 > Synopsis: [patch] krb5 from ports can cause build errors in ftp/curl
 >
 > State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
 > State-Changed-By: sunpoet
 > State-Changed-When: Wed Nov 20 19:47:01 UTC 2013
 > State-Changed-Why:
 > Does this patch [1] work for you?
 >
 > [1] http://people.FreeBSD.org/~sunpoet/curl-pr184041.patch
 >
 >
 > http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=184041


For one thing, you don't need GSSAPI_CPPFLAGS at all.
Just remove it.

As far as the .exists() detection of security/krb5:

 (1) I don't like detection of things like this - I prefer to let the
    user specify the explicit dependency.  We should be getting away
    from .if exists() in ports Makefiles if possible.

    Hence the GSSAPI_BASE option in my patch.  Then the user can
    choose to link either kerberos implemenation (base OS &
    security/krb5 port) and they can both co-exist.

 (2) If you _do_ decide to use this detection, then you need to
    specify security/krb5 as a LIB_DEPEND.  Otherwise, since there is
    no registered dependency, someone could remove security/krb5 and
    break their ftp/curl.

 (3) There is another port that installs gssapi/gssapi.h -
    security/heimdal.  In order to register a dependency due to the
    auto-detection, you have to figure out which port is installed,
    security/krb5 or security/heimdal.
